reasoning: Pan Zhanle holds the 100m freestyle long course world record at 46.40s, a mark set in July 2024 that is significantly faster than any other swimmer in history (nearly 0.5 seconds ahead of the previous non-Pan record). Given this massive gap, the base rate for 100m freestyle records being broken is very low in the short term, especially in the absence of a major global championship (the 2025 World Championships concluded in Singapore, and the next isn't until 2027). Furthermore, doping adjudications that might strip a record typically take years to complete from the time of a positive test, and there is currently no evidence of an active doping investigation against him. Therefore, it is overwhelmingly likely he will remain the record holder on both resolution dates. I have capped probabilities near the maximum permitted bounds, decreasing slightly for the later date to account for any unforeseen events like a sudden shock swim at a minor meet or Wikipedia list vandalism.
